ogTitle: Coneac vs Congarinni North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Coneac and Congarinni North.
heading: Coneac vs Congarinni North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coneac, New South Wales and Congarinni North, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coneac has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Congarinni North (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coneac or Congarinni North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coneac has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Congarinni North scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coneac scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Congarinni North scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coneac or Congarinni North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coneac has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.72% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
